data.title: EMC Gold Issues New CDIs and Reaffirms Compliance as Salave Project Advances
data.description:

Black Dragon Gold Corp. Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 1 Sh ( (AU:EM3) ) has shared an announcement.

EMC Gold Corporation has issued 700,000 Chess Depositary Interest shares at $0.03 each following the conversion of unlisted options, modestly expanding its equity base. The company states the shares were issued without a disclosure document under Australian corporations law while confirming ongoing compliance with financial reporting and continuous disclosure obligations, and that there is no excluded information investors would reasonably require beyond current disclosures.

The announcement underscores EMC Gold’s adherence to regulatory standards as it advances the Salave gold project in Spain, which hosts measured, indicated and inferred mineral resources totalling several million tonnes and over 1.5 million ounces of gold. With additional exploration potential across its Spanish landholdings and its Ivan Well permit in Western Australia, the company continues to position itself as a growing gold exploration player in both European and Australian markets.

More about Black Dragon Gold Corp. Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 1 Sh

EMC Gold Corporation is an ASX-listed exploration company with a global portfolio of mineral exploration assets focused on gold. Its flagship asset is the 100%-owned Salave project in Asturias, northern Spain, considered one of Europe’s largest undeveloped gold projects, complemented by the Ivan Well exploration permit in Western Australia acquired via Marlee Gold Pty Ltd.

Average Trading Volume: 981,741

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$68.7M

data.title: Locate Technologies sets Q3 FY26 investor webinar to detail quarterly performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Locate Technologies Limited ( (NZ:LOC) ) is now available.

Locate Technologies Limited, an NZX-listed AI-driven delivery and logistics technology provider, operates the Locate2u route optimisation and delivery management platform and the Zoom2u on-demand courier marketplace across Australia. Its software offers route planning, delivery management and real-time visibility to fleets, retailers and logistics providers globally, and the group holds Bitcoin as a reserve asset in addition to its core operating businesses, positioning it as New Zealand’s first listed Bitcoin treasury company.

The company will release its Q3 FY26 investor update on 23 April 2026 and host an online investor webinar the same day, led by CEO and founder Steve Orenstein. The session will cover Q3 operational and financial performance across Locate2u and Zoom2u, with a Q&A and a recording to be made available on the company’s investor website, underscoring Locate’s efforts to engage shareholders and provide transparency on its quarterly results.

More about Locate Technologies Limited

Locate Technologies Limited is an AI-powered delivery and logistics technology company listed on the NZX under the ticker LOC. It operates two complementary platforms: Locate2u, a global route optimisation and delivery management platform for fleet operators, retailers and logistics providers, and Zoom2u, an on-demand courier and delivery marketplace in Australia that uses AI to manage dispatch and logistics. The group’s platforms provide route planning, delivery management and real-time visibility for businesses of all sizes, and the company also holds Bitcoin as a reserve asset alongside its operating businesses, making it New Zealand’s first listed Bitcoin treasury company.

Average Trading Volume: 14,322

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: N$15.36M

data.title: Ichiyoshi Securities Sees Profit Surge on Shift to Stable Fee-Based Business
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ichiyoshi Securities Co ( (JP:8624) ) has shared an update.

Ichiyoshi Securities has reported strong preliminary consolidated earnings for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, with operating revenue rising to ¥24.58 billion and net income attributable to owners of the parent jumping to ¥4.39 billion. These figures represent year-on-year gains of 30.7% in operating revenue and 180.8% in net income, driving earnings per share up to ¥137.32.

Management attributed the performance to continued progress in shifting toward a “Stock Type Business Model,” which boosted balances in fund-wrap accounts and investment trusts and increased stable commission income. The company noted that profits surged across all levels as a result, though it cautioned that final results due on April 28 may differ from the preliminary figures given the inherent volatility of the financial instruments-trading business.

More about Ichiyoshi Securities Co

Ichiyoshi Securities Co., Ltd. is a Japanese securities firm listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime market under stock code 8624. The company operates in the financial instruments-trading business, focusing on brokerage and investment services, including fund-wrap accounts and investment trust products aimed at generating stable, fee-based revenue.

Average Trading Volume: 240,443

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen48.2B

Olympio Metals shareholders approved all resolutions at a general meeting held in Perth, confirming broad support for the company’s recent and planned capital management activities. The passed resolutions ratify prior issues of placement shares, approve the issue of a second tranche of placement shares, and authorise new options to placement participants, corporate advisers and project partners, reinforcing the company’s funding structure and strategic project agreements.

data.title: Lupin’s U.S. Arm Settles Humana Antitrust Claim for $30 Million
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Lupin Limited ( (IN:LUPIN) ).

Lupin’s U.S. subsidiary, Lupin Pharmaceuticals, Inc., has been a defendant in consolidated civil antitrust litigation in Philadelphia over alleged anticompetitive conduct involving certain generic drug products. While the company maintains that the allegations are unfounded, it has opted to resolve one strand of the dispute with plaintiff Humana Inc. through a negotiated settlement.

Under the settlement, Lupin Pharmaceuticals will pay USD 30 million to Humana in exchange for a full and final release of all claims against the company and its affiliated entities, with the payment already provisioned in Lupin’s consolidated financial results. The agreement, which follows similar settlements by other defendants, allows Lupin to avoid further legal costs and uncertainty without admitting liability, limiting financial surprise for investors and providing greater clarity around one aspect of its U.S. legal exposures.

More about Lupin Limited

Lupin Limited is an India-headquartered global pharmaceutical company operating through subsidiaries such as Lupin Pharmaceuticals, Inc. in the U.S. It focuses on generic and specialty medicines, serving regulated markets including the United States, and is listed on both the National Stock Exchange of India and BSE Limited.

Average Trading Volume: 30,261

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: 1069.4B INR

See more data about LUPIN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

29metals Ltd. ( (AU:29M) ) just unveiled an announcement.

29Metals Limited has notified the market of the issuance of 18,222,692 unquoted performance rights under its employee incentive scheme. The new securities, designated as 29MAA performance rights and issued on 14 April 2026, are not intended to be quoted on the ASX, underscoring the company’s continued use of equity-linked rewards to incentivise staff and potentially influence future dilution and capital structure.

The issuance highlights 29Metals’ focus on long-term employee alignment with company performance, a common practice among ASX-listed resource companies seeking to retain key talent in a competitive market. While the performance rights do not immediately affect the company’s free float, they represent a contingent claim on future equity that existing shareholders and prospective investors may factor into assessments of ownership and dilution risk.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:29M) stock is a Hold with a A$0.26 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on 29metals Ltd. stock, see the AU:29M Stock Forecast page.

More about 29metals Ltd.

29Metals Limited is an Australian-listed resources company focused on base metals mining and production. The company’s securities trade on the ASX under the code 29M, and it uses equity-based incentive structures to align employees and executives with shareholder value in the mining sector.

Ozz Resources Ltd ( (AU:OZZ) ) has provided an announcement.

Ozz Resources Ltd has applied to the ASX for quotation of 26,637,458 new ordinary fully paid shares, with an issue date of 17 April 2026. The securities form part of a previously announced transaction, and their quotation will expand the company’s listed share base, potentially enhancing liquidity for shareholders and supporting its ongoing corporate and project funding activities.

The application under Appendix 2A formalizes the transition of these issued securities to quoted status on the ASX, aligning them with existing ordinary shares on the market. This step may improve market visibility and access to capital for Ozz Resources as it progresses its resource projects and executes its broader growth strategy within the mining sector.

More about Ozz Resources Ltd

Ozz Resources Ltd is an Australian-listed company in the resources sector, trading on the ASX under the code OZZ. data.title: China Resources Enterprise Exits Substantial Holding in Comvita
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Comvita ( (CVNZF) ) is now available.

China Resources Enterprise (CRE) has ceased to be a substantial shareholder in Comvita after selling its entire holding of 4,408,736 ordinary shares, previously representing about 6.25% of Comvita’s issued capital. The block was sold via a crossing at NZ$0.68 per share, for roughly NZ$3.0 million, removing a major institutional holder from the register and potentially altering Comvita’s shareholder base and trading dynamics, with settlement expected on 20 April 2026.

More about Comvita

Comvita Limited is a New Zealand-listed company, trading under ticker CVT, whose shares are held by a range of institutional investors, including international groups such as China Resources Enterprise. The company operates in the listed equity market, where changes in substantial shareholdings can influence liquidity, investor mix, and market perception of its stock.

Learn more about CVNZF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
